race.name: Maratona dles Dolomites
race.tagline: Seven Dolomite passes, 4,230m of climbing, and a lottery you'll pray to win.

vitals.location: La Villa → Corvara, Alta Badia, South Tyrol
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple stations on/near each pass — Enervit gels, Dole fruit, Marlene apples, Segafredo coffee, speck, strudel, tortellini, prosecco at finish
vitals.cutoff_time: Not strict — broom wagon sweeps slowest riders on each pass

climate.description: Valley 20-28°C, summits 8-15°C lower. Dawn start potentially single digits. 63% rain chance. Thunderstorm risk HIGH — afternoon storms common. Mandatory rain jacket enforced (no vests).
challenges: Alpine weather changes rapidly — sunshine to hail in minutes
Summits above 2,200m can feel near-freezing in rain
1989 edition shortened by ice on passes
Mandatory rain jacket — no vests allowed

terrain.surface: Fully paved — high-quality Italian/South Tyrolean mountain roads
features: 7 named Dolomite passes including HC-rated Passo Giau
Mur dl Giat — 370m at 12.4% avg, 19% max before finish
UNESCO World Heritage Dolomite scenery throughout

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Calibration anchor — Tier 1 ceiling. 7 Dolomite passes, UNESCO scenery, lottery from 30K applicants, 39-year heritage. Road surface scored 1 (fresh tarmac = low difficulty). Prestige 5 confirmed (prestige_5_events list). Cultural impact 5 — defines the gran fondo genre.

key_climbs.name: Passo Campolongo
key_climbs.tactical_notes: Warmup climb. Repeated at km 55 after Sella descent.

key_climbs.name: Passo Pordoi
key_climbs.tactical_notes: Highest paved Dolomite pass. Fausto Coppi monument at summit. Pace conservatively — Giau is still ahead.

key_climbs.name: Passo Sella
key_climbs.tactical_notes: Highest point on course. Steepest Sellaronda pass. Stunning Sassolungo views.

key_climbs.name: Passo Gardena
key_climbs.tactical_notes: Easiest pass — active recovery before the queen stage.

key_climbs.name: Passo Giau
key_climbs.tactical_notes: THE queen stage. 29 hairpins. 9.3% average over 10km is relentless. Decisive — this is where the race is won or survived.

key_climbs.name: Passo Falzarego
key_climbs.tactical_notes: WWI battlefield. Combined climb from Pocol — legs are spent by now.

key_climbs.name: Passo Valparola
key_climbs.tactical_notes: Final summit. Short but legs are destroyed. WWII museum. Then Mur dl Giat sting before Corvara.

course_description.character: An operatic cycling pilgrimage through the heart of the Dolomites. Seven named passes, each with its own personality — from the warmup of Campolongo to the soul-crushing 29 hairpins of Giau. The course loops through UNESCO World Heritage landscapes that make even suffering beautiful. Three route options let you choose your level of punishment.
course_description.signature_challenge: Passo Giau — 9.9km at 9.3% average with 29 hairpins. After 80km and four passes already in the legs, this is where dreams and delusions separate.
suffering_zones: Passo Giau km 5-10: 9.3% avg after 80km, legs screaming, 29 hairpins of psychological warfare
Mur dl Giat: 370m at 12.4% avg, 19% max — vicious final sting before Corvara finish
Passo Sella: 7.9% avg comes early but the 12% max gradient kicks when you're not ready

logistics.airport: Venice Marco Polo (VCE, 3hr drive, best for international), Innsbruck (INN, 2hr), Bolzano (BZO, 1.5hr, limited flights)
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book 1 year ahead — Alta Badia hotels prefer full-week bookings during Rider's Week. Tour operator packages EUR 1,399-4,399+. Alternatively, stay in Bolzano/Bressanone and shuttle in.
logistics.transport: Free shuttle buses across Alta Badia villages during Rider's Week. Roads fully closed to all motorized vehicles on race day.
logistics.food: Feed zones serve Enervit nutrition, local specialties (speck, strudel, tortellini), prosecco at finish. Post-race pasta party in Corvara.

history.origin_story: Founded in 1987 to celebrate the 10th anniversary of Cycling Club Alta Badia. Just 166 riders showed up for the first edition. Named in Ladin — the minority Alpine language of the Dolomite valleys — reflecting deep local cultural roots.
notable_moments: 1987: First edition — 166 riders from Alta Badia cycling club
1989: Edition shortened by ice on passes
2005: Lottery system introduced due to overwhelming demand
2025: 32,700 applicants for 8,000 spots — demand still growing after 38 editions
Each edition has a theme — 2026 theme is PAX
history.reputation: National Geographic called it 'one of the biggest, most passionate, and most chaotic bike races on Earth.' Pirelli: 'almost certainly the most scenic bike race in the world.' Rouleur: 'Italy's grandest gran fondo.' The Maratona IS the gran fondo — it defined the format.

biased_opinion.verdict: The one gran fondo you absolutely must ride before you die.
biased_opinion.summary: If cycling had a spiritual pilgrimage, the Maratona would be Mecca. Seven Dolomite passes of escalating beauty and suffering, culminating in the 29 hairpins of Giau that break you physically and reconstruct you emotionally. The lottery system means you can't just buy your way in — you have to earn the right to suffer. EUR 180 entry for a day that includes speck, strudel, tortellini, and prosecco on the summit? Italy understands value.
strengths: Seven named Dolomite passes — each with distinct character
UNESCO World Heritage scenery that makes suffering transcendent
EUR 180 entry is absurd value for what you get (including food, wine, support)
39 years of history — this event predates the modern gran fondo movement
Lottery creates genuine exclusivity — demand outstrips supply 4:1
1,500+ volunteers, mechanical support, full road closures
weaknesses: Lottery means no guaranteed entry — average 4:1 odds
Alta Badia accommodation books 12 months ahead, tour packages expensive
Alpine weather can turn savage — rain jacket is mandatory for a reason
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The Maratona is the undisputed T1 anchor of road cycling events. It's not the hardest (Haute Route), not the longest (Ötztaler), not the most exclusive (Cape Town by sheer numbers) — but it's the most complete package of scenery, suffering, culture, history, and value in the gran fondo world.

final_verdict.one_liner: Seven Dolomite passes, 4,230m of climbing, and a lottery you'll pray to win — the Maratona defines what a gran fondo should be.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you can win the lottery. This is the bucket-list gran fondo. You need solid climbing legs (4,000m+ is no joke) and a willingness to suffer beautifully. The three route options mean you can ride the Sellaronda (55km) as a stunning intro or go full Maratona for the complete experience.
final_verdict.alternatives: Gran Fondo Stelvio (higher single pass, same Dolomite region), Ötztaler Radmarathon (similar altitude, more savage), Nove Colli (flatter Italian classic if Dolomites intimidate you).

citations.label: Passo Giau climb profile and gradient data
Pirelli — 'most scenic bike race in the world'
